verse: Envy and anger shorten life, and anxiety brings on old age before its time. (Sir 30:24)
Sleeplessness over wealth wastes away one’s flesh, and anxiety about it keeps sleep away. (Sir 31:1)
I was contemplating all the labors of men. And I took notice that their endeavors are open to the envy of their neighbor. And so, in this, too, there is emptiness and superfluous anxiety. (Eccl 4:4)
Remove anxiety from your heart, and put away pain from your flesh. For youth and pleasure are vain. (Eccl 11:10)
Anxiety in the heart of a man weighs him down. And with a good word he shall be made glad. (Pro 12:25)
Do not worry about anything. But in all things, with prayer and supplication, with acts of thanksgiving, let your petitions be made known to God. (Phil 4:6)
Do not choose to be anxious about your life, as to what you may eat, nor about your body, as to what you will wear. Life is more than food, and the body is more than clothing. (Luk 12:22-23)
Can any of you by worrying add a single hour to your life? If then you are not able to do such a little thing as that, why do you worry about the rest? (Luk 12:25-26)

I say to you, do not worry about your life, as to what you will eat or what your will drink, nor about your body, as to what you will wear. Is not life more than food, and the body more than clothing? (Matt 6:25-26)
Consider the birds of the air, how they neither sow, nor reap, nor gather into barns, and yet your heavenly Father feeds them. Are you not of much greater value than they are? (Matt 6:26)

Can any of you by worrying add a single hour to your life? why do you worry about clothing? Consider the lilies of the field, how they grow; they do not toil, neither do they spin, and yet I say to you that not even Solomon in all his glory was clothed like one of them. (Matt 6:27-29)
If God so clothes the grass of the field, which grows today and is thrown into the oven tomorrow, will he not much more clothe you - you of little faith? (Matt 6:30)

Do not choose to be anxious, saying: ‘What shall we eat, or what shall we drink, or with what shall we be clothed?’ For the Gentiles seek all these things. For your Father knows that you need all these things. (Matt 6:31-32)
Do not worry about tomorrow; because tomorrow will have its own worries. Today’s trouble is enough for today. (Matt 6:34)

Are not five sparrows sold for two small coins? And yet not one of these is forgotten before God. But even the very hairs of your head have all been numbered. Therefore, do not be afraid. You are of more value than many sparrows. (Luk 12:6-7)

Woe to those who go down to Egypt for help and who depend on horses, who trust in chariots because they are many and in horsemen because they are very strong, but they look not to the Holy One of Israel or seek the Lord! (Is 31:1)
Cast all your anxieties on him, for he cares for you. (1 Pet 5:7)
Let not your heart be troubled. Believe in God, believe also in me. (Jn 14:1)
He will not permit your foot to be moved; he who keeps you will not slumber. Behold, he who keeps Israel will neither slumber nor sleep. (Ps 121:3-4)
The Lord is your keeper; the Lord is your shade on your right hand. The sun shall not smite you by day, nor the moon by night. (Ps 121:5-6)
The Lord will keep you from all evil; he will keep your life. The Lord will guard your going out and your coming in from this time forth and even for evermore. (Ps 121:7-8)
Thus said the Lord God, the Holy One of Israel: In returning and rest you shall be saved; in quietness and in trust shall be your strength. (Is 30:15)

Blessed is the man who trusts in the Lord, and whose trust the Lord is. He will be like a tree planted by the waters, which sends out its roots by the river. It will not fear when the heat arrives. And its leaves will be green. In the time of drought, it will not be anxious, nor will it cease at any time to bear fruit. (Jer 17:7-8)
My soul waits in silence for God alone; from him comes my salvation. He alone is my rock and my salvation, my defense; I shall never be moved. (Ps 62:1-2)
Trust in him at all times, O people; pour out your heart before him; God is a refuge for us. (Ps 62:8)
Some trust in chariots, and some in horses, but our trust is in the name of the Lord our God. They will be brought down and fall, but we will rise and stand upright. (Ps 20:7)
May the Lord answer you in the day of trouble! May the name of the God of Jacob protect you! May he send you help from the sanctuary and strengthen you from Zion. (Ps 20:1-2)

Behold, the Lord God will come with might, and his arm shall rule for him; his reward is with him, and his work is before him. He will feed his flock like a shepherd; he will gather the lambs in his arms, and carry them in his bosom, and will gently lead those that are young. (Is 40:10-11)

I know that the Lord will maintain the cause of the afflicted and execute justice for the needy. Surely the righteous shall give thanks to your name; the upright shall dwell in your presence. (Ps 140:12-13)
There are many plans in a man’s mind, but it is the Lord’s purpose that will be established. (Pro 19:21)
I wait for the Lord, my soul waits, and in his word I hope; my soul waits for the Lord more than those who watch for the morning, more than those who watch for the morning. (Ps 130:5-6)
The Lord is gracious and righteous; our God is merciful. The Lord preserves the simple; when I was helpless, he saved me. (Ps 116:5-6)
Seek the Lord and his strength; seek his presence at all times. Remember his marvelous works that he has done, his wonders and the judgments he has pronounced. (Ps 105:4-5)
Know that the Lord is God. It is he that made us, and we are his; we are his people, and the sheep of his pasture. (Ps 100:3)
Light shines the righteous, and gladness for the upright in heart. Rejoice in the LORD, you righteous ones, and give thanks to his holy name! (Ps 97:11-12)
If the Lord had not been my help, my soul would soon have dwelt in the abode of silence. When I thought, “My foot is slipping,” your mercy, O Lord, held me up. (Ps 94:17-18)
The Lord will not cast off his people, neither he will not abandon his inheritance. (Ps 94:14)
The righteous thrive like the palm tree and grow like a cedar in Lebanon. They are planted in the house of the Lord; they shall flourish in the courts of our God. (Ps 92:12-13)

Yet a little while, and the wicked will be no more; though you shall look carefully for their place, they will not be there. But the humble shall inherit the land and shall delight themselves in abundance of prosperity. (Ps 37:10-11)
For I know the plans I have for you, says the Lord, plans for welfare and not for disaster, to give you a future of hope. (Jer 29:11)
Trust in the Lord with all your heart, and do not depend upon your own understanding. In all your ways, acknowledge him, and he himself will direct your steps. (Pro 3:5-6)
You will keep in perfect peace, whose mind is stayed on you, because they trust in you. Trust in the Lord for ever, for the Lord God is an everlasting rock. (Is 26:3-4)
The path of the righteous is smooth; O upright One, you make level the way of the righteous. (Is 26:7)
The Lord is good, a stronghold in the day of trouble; he takes care of those who take refuge in him. (Nah 1:7)
Commit your way unto the Lord; trust in him, and he will bring it to pass. He will make your uprightness shine like the light, and your justice like the noonday. (Ps 37:5-6)
Commit your works unto the Lord, and your plans will be established. (Pro 16:3)
I will instruct you and teach you the way you should go; I will guide you with my eye upon you. (Ps 32:8)

The Lord will guide you continually, and satisfy your needs in dry places, and make strong your bones; and you shall be like a watered garden, like a fountain of water, whose waters do not fail. (Is 58:11)
Our soul waits for the Lord; he is our help and our shield. Our hearts rejoice in him, because we trust in his holy name. (Ps 33:20-21)
We know that all things work together unto good, for those who love God, who are called in accordance with his purpose. (Rom 8:28)
